summary: Found 3 critical bugs in cascade delete and authorization: (1) 404/403 information leak on DELETE /groups/:id allows group enumeration, (2) Can create recipes with non-existent groupIds violating FK integrity, (3) Inconsistent error codes for authorization vs not found. Most cascade operations work correctly.

bugs.actual: 404 Not Found (reveals that resource exists and only authorization issue)
bugs.proposed_fix: Check authorization before querying database. Return 403 immediately if user_id doesn't match, not 404 when group not found
bugs.expected: 403 Forbidden or 401 Unauthorized (consistent with authorization failure)
bugs.attack_description: DELETE request to other user's group returns 404 instead of 403/401, revealing resource existence
bugs.reproduction:
1. User A creates group (ID: grpA_id)
2. User B tries: DELETE /groups/{grpA_id}
3. Result: 404 (WRONG - should be 403)
Compare with: User B tries DELETE /groups/{fake_id} -> also 404
Attacker can enumerate existing groups by status code
bugs.file_hint: DELETE /groups/:id endpoint - missing authorization check before 404

bugs.actual: 201 Created - recipe created with invalid groupIds that reference deleted/non-existent groups
bugs.proposed_fix: Validate all groupIds in request exist and belong to current user before creating recipe. Use transaction to ensure consistency.
bugs.expected: 400 Bad Request or 404 Not Found (group doesn't exist)
bugs.attack_description: Can create recipe with non-existent groupIds, violating referential integrity
bugs.file_hint: POST /recipes endpoint - missing validation of groupIds existence

bugs.actual: 404 for both non-existent and unauthorized groups
bugs.proposed_fix: Always return 403 Forbidden for any DELETE request on resources not owned by user, regardless of existence
bugs.expected: 403 Forbidden for all unauthorized deletes (whether group exists or not)
bugs.attack_description: DELETE request to non-existent group returns 404, same as unauthorized access, enabling user enumeration
bugs.reproduction:
curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer TOKEN_USER_B' -X DELETE https://api/groups/GROUP_ID_OF_USER_A
Returns 404 - attacker can't distinguish between 'doesn't exist' and 'not authorized'
